features.description: The Xcode project can be opened and built by a fresh developer or agent session.
acceptance: Run ./init.sh from the repository root.
Verify xcodebuild lists the Parrot scheme.
Verify the Debug macOS build completes with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO.
features.notes: ./init.sh completed successfully in the current environment. The harness now builds into the repo-local ./.DerivedData path to avoid multiple system DerivedData app copies during TCC-sensitive debugging.

features.description: A fresh agent can understand startup, current progress, and next work without prior chat history.
acceptance: Read parrot-progress.md for current state and next steps.
Read feature_list.json for implementation priorities and verification criteria.
Use ./init.sh for project metadata and build verification.
features.notes: Initial handoff files are present; keep them updated after feature work. AGENTS.md and parrot-progress.md document ./init.sh --run as the stable local debug entry point: stop old Parrot instances, build into ./.DerivedData, and open that exact app bundle.

features.description: The project can produce GitHub-style unsigned macOS Release packages from SemVer-tagged commits.
acceptance: Document the SemVer, Git tag, and GitHub Release asset workflow.
Provide a repeatable script that builds an unsigned Release app.
Generate both .dmg and .zip assets under Dist/.
Generate SHA256SUMS.txt and GitHub-style RELEASE_NOTES.md.
Reject formal releases when MARKETING_VERSION, the current tag, or the git worktree state is invalid.
Allow clearly labeled local dev packaging before a release tag exists.
features.notes: Added Docs/release-process.md and Scripts/package-release.sh. The release process documents SemVer, v<MARKETING_VERSION> tags, GitHub Release assets, Gatekeeper warnings, checksum verification, and no-retag safety rules. The packaging script reads MARKETING_VERSION, CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION, and PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER from Config/Release.xcconfig, enforces SemVer, rejects formal releases from dirty worktrees or non-matching tags, supports local --allow-untagged dev packages, builds Release with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO, verifies app bundle identifier/version/build metadata, ad-hoc signs the full app bundle for local resource-seal integrity with a stable local designated requirement based on the bundle identifier, verifies codesign --deep --strict before packaging, rejects pure cdhash designated requirements so Screen Recording TCC grants are not tied to a per-build hash, detects architecture, and creates .dmg, .zip, SHA256SUMS.txt, and RELEASE_NOTES.md under Dist/. Follow-up fix for v0.1.2-alpha Gatekeeper 'app is damaged' dialog: the previous package had only executable-level ad-hoc/linker signing and no sealed resources, causing codesign verification to fail with 'code has no resources but signature indicates they must be present'. Follow-up fix for v0.1.3-alpha Screen Recording permission loop: the previous pure ad-hoc release had designated requirement cdhash-only, which produced duplicate Parrot.app entries in System Settings and allowed users to enable an old hash while the current app remained unauthorized. Verified bash -n Scripts/package-release.sh, git diff --check, full Scripts/package-release.sh --allow-untagged Release build, shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS.txt for the generated dmg and zip, mounted the generated DMG and verified Parrot.app with codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=4, confirmed the generated Release app, DMG app, ZIP app, and installed /Applications/Parrot.app all report designated => identifier "com.example.parrot", confirmed the DMG app has Sealed Resources version 2 and CodeResources, confirmed spctl still rejects only because there is no Developer ID signature, and unzipped the ZIP artifact to verify the packaged app signature. Real System Settings inspection on 2026-06-23 confirmed two Parrot.app Screen Recording entries from old cdhash identities, matching the reported restart-still-unauthorized symptom.

features.description: High-fidelity product prototypes are available as implementation references.
acceptance: Store the provided prototype images under Design/ with descriptive filenames.
Document each prototype surface and its intended implementation reference.
Keep the prototypes discoverable from the harness source documents.
features.notes: Added four high-fidelity prototype images and Design/README.md.

features.description: The app runs as a macOS menu-bar utility with entries for key actions.
acceptance: Launch the app and verify a menu-bar item appears.
Open settings from the menu-bar item.
Open quick text translation from the menu-bar item.
Trigger screenshot translation from the menu-bar item.
Quit the app from the menu-bar item.
features.notes: Added an AppKit NSStatusItem with Quick Text Translation, Screenshot Translation, Pause Shortcuts, Settings, and Quit Parrot menu entries. The SwiftUI app no longer opens a default WindowGroup on launch, so cold start shows the status item without an extra main window that can be confused with the status menu. Verified via Accessibility that the status menu lists all expected entries and that Quick Text Translation and Settings open their windows.

features.description: The app supports global shortcuts for screenshot translation and quick text translation.
acceptance: Configure default shortcuts for screenshot translation and text translation.
Verify shortcuts work while another app is frontmost.
Verify shortcuts can be paused or disabled.
Surface an actionable error if required accessibility permissions are missing.
features.notes: Verified with automated GUI smoke test using Swift CGEvent and Accessibility APIs. Finder was frontmost when Cmd+Shift+T opened Quick Text Translation and Cmd+Shift+2 opened Screenshot Translation. AXPress verified Pause Shortcuts changes the menu to Resume Shortcuts and prevents both shortcuts from opening windows, then Resume Shortcuts restores both shortcuts. Debug build succeeded with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO. Carbon HotKey registration does not require Accessibility permission for the app itself; Accessibility was only used by the test harness automation.

features.description: Users can select an arbitrary screen region for screenshot translation.
acceptance: Invoke screenshot mode from the shortcut or menu.
Drag to select a region on the active screen.
Cancel selection with Esc.
Pass the selected image region to the OCR pipeline.
features.notes: Added a native AppKit full-screen overlay for region selection. Follow-up fixes made the app an LSUIElement menu-bar utility, removed the default launch WindowGroup, and changed screenshot capture to snapshot every screen before the overlay appears, then crop the selected region from that cached screenshot. Added Screen Recording preflight/request before snapshotting, because macOS can return a desktop-only image when permission is missing. A release-package regression showed CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess can present the macOS Screen Recording prompt while returning false for the current attempt; the app now suppresses the Parrot error window only for the first request-presented state, then shows Parrot's actionable permission guidance on a later attempt if Screen Recording is still not granted. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, screen-capture access gate E2E, and a real Debug app smoke where two Cmd+Shift+2 attempts with Screen Recording still missing left the system '录屏' prompt visible and opened Parrot's Screenshot Translation permission guidance window.

features.description: The app uses local OCR to recognize text from screenshots before translation.
acceptance: Recognize text from a selected screenshot region using local macOS capabilities.
Do not upload screenshot images by default.
Show a clear message when no text is detected.
Preserve paragraph structure where OCR output allows it.
features.notes: Implemented local OCR with Vision VNRecognizeTextRequest. The screenshot image stays local; the pipeline returns recognized text only and shows a clear no-text message when Vision finds no candidates. Paragraph/line structure is preserved by joining recognized observations with newlines. Follow-up optimization for Chinese OCR scales small selections up to 4x before recognition, prioritizes zh-Hans/zh-Hant before en-US, and lowers minimumTextHeight for compact UI text. Verified with ./init.sh Debug build and equivalent smoke tests: English two-line image recognized "Hello Parrot OCR" and "Translate this text"; dark compact mixed Chinese/English image recognized "时间线" and "Cue-Pro".

features.description: Users can configure an OpenAI-compatible LLM provider.
acceptance: Enter Base URL, model name, and API Key.
Test the provider connection.
Show concise errors for invalid credentials, network failure, and timeout.
Avoid hard-coded provider-specific assumptions beyond OpenAI-compatible defaults.
features.notes: Implemented ProviderSettingsView for provider preset, Base URL, model, and API Key entry. Default recommended preset is DeepSeek with https://api.deepseek.com and deepseek-v4-flash, based on https://api-docs.deepseek.com/zh-cn/. Additional presets include GLM with https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4 and glm-4.7, OpenAI, and Custom. Non-secret settings are stored in UserDefaults; API Keys are saved/replaced/deleted via macOS Keychain per provider. Settings now shows an API Key setup guide when the current provider has no non-secret setup record, and the app opens Settings on startup when setup is required. Connection testing uses a freshly entered API Key directly after saving, or performs a non-interactive saved-key read that reports an in-app re-entry error instead of showing a system Keychain password prompt. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, source-linked keychain UX E2E, and a real Debug app smoke where missing setup opens Settings.

features.description: API keys are stored in macOS Keychain and never written to repository files or logs.
acceptance: Save API Key to Keychain.
Read API Key from Keychain for requests.
Delete or replace API Key from settings.
Verify repository files and logs do not contain secret values.
features.notes: KeychainSecretStore is implemented and integrated with settings save, test, replace, and delete flows. API Keys are isolated by provider account suffix so DeepSeek, GLM, OpenAI, and Custom can use different secrets. The app stores only a non-secret provider setup record in UserDefaults, uses that record for Settings/onboarding/missing-key decisions, and never probes Keychain just to decide whether an API Key exists. Quick Text and Screenshot OCR translation now check the setup record before reading any API Key, and readAPIKey also checks the setup record before returning a process-cached secret. Successful saves refresh an in-memory process cache. Translation reads otherwise combine LAContext.interactionNotAllowed=true with kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail so macOS Keychain password prompts are disabled during translation. If a previous debug-build Keychain item still requires system UI, Parrot clears that provider setup record and cached secret, then shows an in-app re-enter-key/missing-key message, so retries do not keep touching Keychain. Repository scan found no token-like API secret values in tracked source. Verified ./init.sh Debug build and Scripts/keychain-cache-e2e.swift covering no-setup no-read, Settings no-read, save/cache/delete, setup-record-gates-cache, authentication-UI-fail enforcement, unsafe-record cleanup, and retry-after-cleanup no-read.

features.description: Users can summon a lightweight text input window and quickly translate pasted or typed text.
acceptance: Open the text translation window from a global shortcut.
Focus the input field automatically.
Translate with Enter or the chosen primary action.
Close the transient window with Esc.
Copy the translation with Cmd+Enter.
features.notes: Implemented QuickTextTranslationView and wired the existing Quick Text Translation menu/global shortcut entry to a real keyboard-first input window. The window auto-focuses a native NSTextView, Enter triggers translation through the configured OpenAI-compatible provider, Esc closes, Cmd+K clears, and Cmd+Enter copies the translated text and closes after a successful result. Follow-up fix changed translation from a one-shot /chat/completions request to OpenAI-compatible SSE streaming so Translation receives text deltas as they arrive; the ready message is only set after the stream finishes, and errors clear partial output and show the exception message. A repeated empty Translation-area report showed the first follow-up was incomplete: the result was still rendered by a dynamic SwiftUI ScrollView/Text inside a too-small fixed window, so the same StateObject data could appear only after a later window relayout. The result area now uses a fixed-height AppKit read-only NSTextView that is updated directly from translatedText, and the quick translation window height is increased to fit the result area. Verified ./init.sh Debug build and checked no other Swift caller still uses the old non-streaming translate path. User local E2E verification on 2026-06-22 confirmed Cmd+Shift+T opens the quick translation window, text input translates with streaming output in the Translation area, the ready message appears only after translation completes, and Cmd+Enter copies the translation and closes the window.

features.description: The app automatically translates Chinese input to English and English input to Chinese.
acceptance: Translate Chinese source text into English.
Translate English source text into Chinese.
Preserve paragraphs, code, variable names, links, product names, and proper nouns.
Do not add explanations unless the user explicitly asks for explanation mode.
features.notes: Current implementation auto-detects target language from source text: Chinese text translates to English, otherwise text translates to Simplified Chinese. The shared translation prompt preserves paragraph structure, code, variable names, links, product names, and proper nouns, and asks the provider to output only the translation without explanations. Marked passing based on implementation review after Quick Text Translation and screenshot comparison flows were already verified with the shared streaming translation path.

features.description: Translation results are displayed in a lightweight original/translated comparison window.
acceptance: Show OCR or input text and translated text in a readable comparison layout.
Provide copy translated text and copy original text actions.
Allow retry when translation fails.
Close the result window with Esc.
features.notes: Implemented screenshot OCR result as a lightweight original/translated comparison window. OCR success now auto-starts OpenAI-compatible streaming translation using the same translateStreaming path as Quick Text Translation, so translated text appears incrementally. The window shows the screenshot preview, original OCR text, translated text, copy translation, copy original, retry, and Esc/Close handling. The text panes use native AppKit read-only NSTextView wrappers to avoid first-render blank result issues seen in the quick translation window. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, and feature_list.json JSON validation. User local E2E verification on 2026-06-22 confirmed the screenshot translation comparison window works with real screenshot selection, configured provider streaming response, copy actions, retry, and Esc close.

features.description: The app surfaces clear user-facing errors for permission, OCR, authentication, network, and timeout failures.
acceptance: Show missing permission guidance with next-step actions.
Show no-text-detected OCR failures without crashing.
Show authentication failures without exposing secrets.
Show network and timeout failures with retry options.
features.notes: Implemented actionable user-facing errors across the P0 flows. Screenshot capture no longer stacks a Parrot error window on top of the first macOS Screen Recording permission prompt; if the user triggers screenshot capture again while Screen Recording is still not granted, the app now opens the Screenshot Capture Failed guidance window instead of silently doing nothing. OCR no-text/unavailable/failure statuses stay in the screenshot result window and include a New Screenshot action so the app does not crash or strand the user. Provider errors are mapped into concise titles, messages, and recovery suggestions; authentication messages redact token-like secrets and provider messages are truncated/sanitized before display. Quick Text Translation now has an explicit Retry button after failures, while screenshot translation keeps its Retry action for network/timeout/provider errors. Verified with ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, CGEvent smoke that Cmd+Shift+T opens Quick Text Translation, and an equivalent integration/E2E executable compiled from app sources that covered authentication redaction, network and timeout retry guidance, invalid HTTPS Base URL guidance, and blank-image OCR no-text handling. Follow-up checks added Scripts/screen-capture-access-gate-e2e.swift and verified a real Debug app with two Cmd+Shift+2 attempts: the first keeps only the system '录屏' prompt, and the second opens Parrot's Screenshot Translation permission guidance while permission is still missing.

features.description: Users can view recent translation history and disable history if desired.
acceptance: Store recent text translation records when history is enabled.
Open history from the menu-bar item.
Copy previous translations.
Disable history from settings.
features.notes: Implemented local text-only translation history. Successful Quick Text and Screenshot translations add records through TranslationHistoryStore when history is enabled. Records are stored as JSON under Application Support/Parrot/translation-history.json, capped to the most recent 50 entries, and never include screenshot images or API Keys. Added a menu-bar Translation History window with recent original/translation pairs, Copy Translation, Copy Original, Clear History, and Esc/Close. Added a Save translation history toggle in Settings; disabling it prevents new records from being saved while leaving existing records available until cleared. Follow-up UI fix keeps each history card at a stable summary height, truncates long original/translation previews, and adds a View Details sheet with scrollable full original and translation text so long content cannot overlap the next record. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, a source-linked E2E executable (Scripts/translation-history-e2e.swift) covering default enablement, JSON persistence/reload, disabled-save behavior, max-count trimming, clear, and NSPasteboard copy, plus real app smoke tests via ./init.sh --run and status menu AX click that opened the Translation History window. A screenshot check with long records confirmed the summary cards no longer overflow into the next record; coordinate-click detail validation was blocked by macOS Accessibility for osascript in this environment.

features.description: Users can customize screenshot and text translation shortcuts.
acceptance: Record user-selected shortcut combinations.
Persist shortcut preferences.
Detect conflicts or invalid shortcuts.
Apply changed shortcuts without restarting when practical.
Expose shortcut preferences from the unified Settings window alongside existing Model and Privacy settings.
features.notes: Implemented custom shortcuts for the two existing global actions only: Quick Text Translation and Screenshot Translation. ShortcutPreferences persists Codable keyboard descriptors in UserDefaults with defaults Cmd+Shift+T and Cmd+Shift+2. The unified Settings window now exposes Model, Shortcuts, and Privacy sections; Shortcuts includes native key recording, per-action validation, conflict detection, Restore Defaults, and Save Shortcuts. Saving valid shortcuts calls GlobalShortcutManager.reloadShortcuts() so Carbon hot keys are re-registered without restarting when the Settings window is used. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, source-linked E2E Scripts/custom-shortcuts-e2e.swift for defaults/persistence/conflicts/invalid Shift-only combinations/reset behavior, and a real App smoke test: preloaded Cmd+Option+Y for Quick Text, launched with ./init.sh --run, sent the shortcut while Finder was frontmost, and observed the Quick Text Translation window. Also verified the status menu opens the unified Settings window. The test override was removed after verification.

features.description: Users can open the Settings window from a dedicated global shortcut.
acceptance: Expose Open Settings as a configurable action in the Shortcuts settings section.
Provide a default shortcut recommendation of Cmd+Option+, without removing the existing menu-bar Settings entry.
Persist the shortcut preference and detect conflicts with Quick Text Translation, Screenshot Translation, and invalid combinations.
Apply the changed settings shortcut without restarting when practical.
Verify the shortcut opens Settings while another app is frontmost.
features.notes: Implemented Open Settings as the third configurable GlobalShortcutAction with default Cmd+Option+,. The Shortcuts settings section now exposes Quick Text Translation, Screenshot Translation, and Open Settings with the existing recorder, persistence, conflict/invalid validation, Restore Defaults, and save-time hot-key reload path. Existing two-action ShortcutPreferences decode with the new Open Settings default so older user custom shortcuts are preserved. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, source-linked Scripts/custom-shortcuts-e2e.swift covering defaults/persistence/conflicts/invalid combinations/reset/legacy migration, and a real Debug app smoke: launched via ./init.sh --run, confirmed no Parrot windows, activated Finder, sent Cmd+Option+, via CGEvent, and observed owner=Parrot name=Settings.

features.description: Users can choose source and target languages in Quick Text and Screenshot translation surfaces, then swap them with one action.
acceptance: Show a lightweight source language and target language control in Quick Text Translation.
Show the same language controls in the Screenshot Translation result window.
Support Auto source language and Auto Opposite target language while preserving the current Chinese-English default behavior.
Support at least Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, French, and Spanish as explicit language choices.
Support one-click source-target swap and use the swapped direction for retry or retranslate.
Prevent saving or translating with the same explicit source and target language.
features.notes: Planned in Docs/ai-translation-macos-v1-prd.md. This replaces the hidden hard-coded Chinese-English target detection with explicit TranslationPreferences that still defaults to the current behavior. Verification must prove the selected direction changes the actual prompt sent to the provider.

features.description: Users can choose a translation style such as accurate, natural, professional, or concise.
acceptance: Expose translation style in the Translation settings section.
Default the style to Accurate to preserve current MVP behavior.
Support Accurate, Natural, Professional, and Concise style options.
Apply the selected style to both Quick Text and Screenshot translation prompts.
Allow retranslation after changing style without requiring users to re-enter text or retake a screenshot.
features.notes: Planned in Docs/ai-translation-macos-v1-prd.md. Keep this as a low-friction preference before exposing raw Prompt editing to most users.

features.description: Users can view the default translation Prompt and optionally enable a custom Prompt template.
acceptance: Show the full default translation Prompt in the Translation settings section.
Allow users to enable or disable a custom Prompt template.
Validate that custom templates include required variables for target language and text before saving.
Provide a Restore Default action that returns translation to the built-in Prompt behavior.
Fall back to the default Prompt if the saved custom Prompt becomes invalid.
Verify Quick Text and Screenshot translation both use the active Prompt template.
features.notes: Planned in Docs/ai-translation-macos-v1-prd.md. The initial required variables are {target_language} and {text}; supported variables also include {source_language}, {style}, and {glossary}. Do not log Prompt contents if they may contain private user wording.

features.description: Users can maintain a local personal terminology glossary that translation prompts obey when source text matches a term.
acceptance: Provide a local glossary UI in the Translation settings section.
Support adding, editing, deleting, enabling, and disabling glossary entries.
Require source term and target term fields, with optional target language and context fields.
Prevent duplicate source terms for the same target language.
Inject only glossary entries that match the current source text into the translation Prompt.
Verify a matched glossary term changes the translation output or request Prompt while unmatched terms are not sent.
features.notes: Planned in Docs/ai-translation-macos-v1-prd.md. Keep the glossary local and personal for V1. Do not build team sharing, cloud sync, or large TBX/TMX import in this feature.

features.description: Users can edit OCR-recognized source text before retranslating a screenshot result.
acceptance: Show the OCR-recognized text in an editable state from the Screenshot Translation result window.
Allow users to edit the recognized text and retranslate without taking a new screenshot.
Use the edited text, not the original OCR text, for the provider request.
Save the edited source text in translation history when history is enabled.
Keep screenshot images local and do not add image persistence.
features.notes: Planned in Docs/ai-translation-macos-v1-prd.md and aligned with the original MVP PRD's OCR failure recovery path. This should be implemented after language controls so retranslation uses the same shared translation request model.

features.description: Users can choose where lightweight translation windows appear.
acceptance: Expose floating window position preference in Settings after the Translation settings foundation exists.
Support Screen Center, Mouse Nearby, and Last Position options.
Keep Quick Text Translation defaulting to Screen Center.
Keep Screenshot Translation preferring the screenshot region nearby when space allows, with a visible-screen fallback.
Persist the preference without changing privacy behavior or storing screenshot images.
features.notes: Planned in Docs/ai-translation-macos-v1-prd.md. Lower priority than settings shortcut, language controls, Prompt, glossary, and OCR text editing because it affects comfort more than translation correctness.
